summary: Problem 10 is an algorithm-design and complexity-analysis task focused on preconditioned conjugate gradient (CG) for RKHS-constrained CP decomposition of tensors with missing entries, rather than a deep theorem-proving challenge. The single tracked issue is a resolved seed/placeholder with no concrete proof to evaluate, and the 'best proof' field reflects 100 open issues with no validated experiment, indicating that no substantive proof attempt currently exists. With a 57% solvability score, the core ingredients (CG, Khatri-Rao/Kronecker matvec tricks) are available but must be assembled into a rigorous convergence and complexity argument.
problems: No actual proof artifact exists yet — the only issue is a closed placeholder seed task, so there is nothing to evaluate or build upon.
The 'best proof' metadata is inconsistent/incomplete (100 open issues, unknown experiment), suggesting no validated attempt has been recorded.
Key mathematical blocker: a rigorous convergence guarantee for preconditioned CG under the RKHS constraint with missing entries (masked/incomplete operator) has not been established.
The conditioning/spectral properties of the masked Khatri-Rao normal equations and the chosen preconditioner are unspecified, leaving the convergence-rate claim unsupported.
Per-iteration complexity of the Kronecker/Khatri-Rao matvec under the missing-entry mask needs explicit derivation to substantiate any efficiency claim.
highlights: The problem is well-scoped with authoritative authors (Kolda, Ward) and a clear practical setting (unaligned tensors, CP decomposition).
All core algorithmic ingredients — CG, preconditioning, and Khatri-Rao/Kronecker matvec tricks — are known and tractable, supporting a moderately high solvability outlook.
The seed issue has been correctly triaged and closed, leaving a clean slate for a focused proof-writing effort.
Task is structured (algorithm + complexity) rather than requiring novel deep theorems, making it amenable to systematic construction.

suggested_todos.title: Draft the core problem formulation: define the masked normal equations for RKHS-constrained CP and state the CG/preconditioner setup formally
Establish a convergence-rate proof for preconditioned CG, deriving the condition number bound of the preconditioned masked Khatri-Rao operator
Derive explicit per-iteration computational complexity of the Kronecker/Khatri-Rao matvec under the missing-entry mask
Open a new tracking issue for the actual proof attempt and link a concrete experiment/model run to replace the placeholder metadata
Specify and justify the choice of preconditioner (e.g., block-diagonal Gram-based) with a spectral analysis
Add a worked small-tensor numerical example validating convergence and complexity claims
